name: Scottish Ginger Cake
recipeInstructions:
A rich cake that improves with keeping in an airtight tin. Very nice served with tea.
Preheat oven to 325°F (165°C). Generously grease an 8-inch square baking pan; set aside. Sift flour, salt, baking soda, and ground ginger into a large bowl. Stir in raisins (plump by soaking them in sherry or water before using), candied peel, and crystallized ginger; set aside. In a medium-size saucepan, combine molasses, butter, and brown sugar. Stir over low heat until butter is melted. Remove from heat. Beat in eggs and milk. Make a well in the center of the reserved dry ingredients. Pour in the molasses mixture and beat thoroughly. Pour into cake pan. Bake 1 1/4 hours, or until the top springs back when lightly touched with a finger. Cool in pan on a wire rack. Makes 8 to 12 servings.
